utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . are you ready to get started ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , susan is a 26-year-old female here for a high blood pressure check . susan , what's going on with your blood pressure ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , i've always struggled with , um , high blood pressure , but it's seeming to get really out of control lately . so , i wanted to come in and see if there's any type of new medication i can be on to help it get steady or if there is diet restrictions i should take , um , things like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , i know that we've had you on the norvasc and we had to increase your dose , so you're on 10 milligrams a day now . and what are your blood pressures running at home ? you c- ... like , have they been like over 150 ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , they have been .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and , have you had any headaches ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have had some headaches . i do n't know if that's just because of more stress at work or , um , because of the high blood pressure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . so , you've had some headaches . have you had any chest pain , shortness of breath , anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , and , you know , i know that you've had a kidney transplant a few years ago .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: everything is okay with that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: everything's been good and solid there .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you're taking all of your auto , um , i- um , your immunosuppression medications ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , let's go ahead and we'll do a quick physical exam . so , looking at you , you do n't appear any distress . your neck is nice and supple . your heart is irregular rate and rhythm . i do hear a slight , uh , two out of six systolic ejection murmur . your lungs are clear . i do see the scar on the left , uh , left plank for your kidney transplant which is healed . and you have no lower extremity edema . so , let's go ahead and look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the creatinine . so , your kidney function looks really good . so , that's really promising after your transplant . hey , dragon , show me the ekg . and your ekg looks great , uh , perfectly normal , so that's good . um , so , my impression of you at this time ... , let me see your blood pressure .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the blood pressure . yeah , and you're right , it-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . it is high . so , there is something that we can do for that . so , let's go ahead and we'll put you on ... so , my impression of you is that your , your blood pressure is high and the norvasc is n't controlling it . and , you know , we can definitely get it under better control . so , what i would like to do is prescribe you coreg 25 milligrams , twice a day . and that will help bring your blood pressure down , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , order carvedilol 25 milligrams , twice a day . uh , i'd also like to go ahead and , uh , get a lipid panel and some routine labs on you just to make sure that everything is okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , order a lipid panel . hey , dragon , order a complete metabolic panel . all right . and then , um , from a transplant standpoint , everything looks fine . your kidney function looks great , so we're just gon na continue you on all of those medications . so , the nurse will come in soon and she'll help you schedule all of the al- schedule a follow-up appointment with me , and we'll see what your blood pressure looks like in a couple weeks after taking that medication . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . thanks , susan .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 26-year-old female presenting for a high blood pressure check.
Hypertension
The doctor asks the patient to describe her current blood pressure concerns.
The patient reports a history of high blood pressure that has recently become uncontrolled and expresses interest in medication or diet changes.
The patient is currently taking Norvasc 10 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient's home blood pressure readings have been over 150.
The patient confirms her home blood pressure readings have been over 150.
The doctor asks if the patient has been experiencing headaches.
The patient reports headaches and is unsure if they are related to work stress or high blood pressure.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced chest pain or shortness of breath.
The patient denies chest pain or shortness of breath.
Kidney transplant
The doctor asks if the patient's post-transplant status is stable.
The patient reports that her post-transplant status is good.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking her immunosuppression medications.
The patient confirms she is taking her immunosuppression medications.
Physical exam findings: no distress, supple neck, 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, healed left flank transplant scar, and no lower extremity edema.
Creatinine and EKG results are normal.
The doctor prescribes carvedilol 25 mg twice a day.
The doctor orders a lipid panel and a complete metabolic panel.
The doctor advises continuing current immunosuppression medications.
The patient will schedule a follow-up appointment in a couple of weeks.

case_description: high blood pressure check; hx kidney transplant
